// Serve one cup every 30 frames if (gameState.servingTimer >= 30) { if (gameState.servingIndex < gameState.player.cups.length) { const cup = gameState.player.cups[gameState.servingIndex]; const customer = gameState.customers[gameState.servingIndex]; customer.serve(); gameState.score += cup.value; gameState.coins += cup.value; gameState.servingIndex++; gameState.servingTimer = 0; } else { // All cups served gameState.gamePhase = 'GAME_OVER_WIN'; p.logs.game_info.push({ data: { gamePhase: "GAME_OVER_WIN", score: gameState.score }, framecount: p.frameCount, timestamp: Date.now() }); } } } function resetGame(p) { // Clear entities gameState.entities.forEach(entity => { if (entity.body) { World.remove(world, entity.body); } }); if (gameState.player) { gameState.player.cups.forEach(cup => { World.remove(world, cup.body); }); } gameState.entities = []; gameState.player = null; gameState.score = 0; gameState.distanceTraveled = 0; gameState.cupsCollected = 0; gameState.obstaclesHit = 0; gameState.gatesPassed = 0; gameState.servingPhase = false; gameState.customers = []; // Keep total coins accumulated across runs } }); // Expose globally window.gameInstance = gameInstance;

Coffee Stack

Navigate your growing stack of coffee cups through a lane-based course! Collect empty cups, pass through gates to fill them with coffee, add sleeves, and attach lids to increase their value. Avoid obstacles that destroy cups from your stack. At the end, serve your customized cups to customers for coins. Earn more coins with larger, fully-customized stacks!

Arrow Left / A: Move stack to the left lane | Arrow Right / D: Move stack to the right lane | Enter: Start the game from the start screen | ESC: Pause/Unpause the game | R: Restart from game over screen

TEST_1.expected_outcome: The stack should successfully move between lanes, collect cups that increase stack size, and lose cups when hitting obstacles. The game should transition to the serving phase when reaching the end. Test passes if lane switching and cup collection work smoothly without errors.

TEST_3.expected_outcome: The game should transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE when all cups are lost. A game over screen should display with the option to restart. Test passes if the game handles empty stack correctly without crashing.

TEST_4.expected_outcome: The game should freeze all movement when paused and display a "PAUSED" overlay. Unpausing should resume exactly where it left off. Test passes if pause/unpause works without state corruption.

TEST_5.strategy_description: Collect cups and then navigate through multiple gates in a specific order: first coffee gate, then sleeve gate, then lid gate. Observe the cup appearance changes after each gate.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Cups should show cumulative customization as they pass through gates. A cup should show coffee fill, then add a sleeve band, then add a lid on top. Test passes if visual customization stacks properly and is reflected in the final coin value.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing edge case of collecting cups after passing through customization gates. This verifies that newly collected cups don't inherit customizations and that the stack maintains separate states for different cups.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Start with a few cups, pass through a coffee gate to customize them, then collect new empty cups afterward. Continue to observe if new cups are empty or inherit customization.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Only cups that physically pass through gates should be customized. New cups collected after gates should be empty. Test passes if customization is applied correctly on a per-cup basis.

TEST_7.strategy_description: Create a stack with mixed customization levels: some empty cups, some with just coffee, some fully customized. Reach the end and observe the coin calculation and serving animation.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Each cup should be served individually with appropriate coin values: 1 for empty, progressively more for customized cups up to 10 for fully customized. The total score should accurately reflect the stack composition. Test passes if scoring is accurate and visible.
